summary: A uniquely spacey 1985 show with standout performances of Sugaree, Terrapin Station and Good Lovin', captured in a recording that falls short of the best available sources.
review: Fans rave about this rare ’85 gig, calling it “super” for its adventurous jams (Bob Jam) and highlighting Jerry’s soaring vocals on Sugaree as well as a fiery second set from Terrapin through Good Lovin'. The audio isn’t a pristine soundboard – listeners note the venue PA was poor and that this tape doesn’t match the quality of the Dave Cohen version – but the musical excitement outweighs the technical shortcomings.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is consistently praised (high 5‑star ratings) while recording quality receives mixed comments, pulling the overall rating slightly below a perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No explicit source type mentioned; reviewers note the venue sound system was poor and that this tape is not as good as the Dave Cohen copy, suggesting modest audio fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is praised for its unique, space‑y first‑set jam (Bob Jam) and a scorching second set that runs from Terrapin through Good Lovin', closing with Keep Your Day Job as an encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His vocals and guitar on Sugaree are highlighted as some of the best ever heard.
